commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9412fb790918841a1a7f50c23a7b377e7f2e5bc1)
commands.review_summary:
Adds Telegram `all_private_chats` to command-menu set/delete scopes and explicit native-disabled cleanup, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +104. Total +110 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only syncs and clears default plus group command scopes, while the Telegram dependency exposes `all_private_chats` as a distinct narrower command scope. I did not run a live Telegram repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining merge blocker is live Telegram proof or maintainer override, which automation cannot supply for the contributor's real environment.

target.title: [codex] fix(telegram): sync native commands to private and group scopes [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram native slash-command menus are now synced and cleared for default, private-chat, and group-chat scopes. This prevents private chats from retaining stale command menus when the bot updates or disables native commands.

AI-assisted by Codex GPT-5.5.

## What Changed

- Added `all_private_chats` to the shared Telegram command-menu scope list.
- Added private-chat cleanup to the explicit native-disabled command cleanup path.
- Kept command construction, authorization, transport behavior, and config unchanged.
- Added focused regression coverage for scope ordering, empty-menu cleanup, cache skips, localized command variants, retry behavior, and native-disabled cleanup.

## Scope Boundaries

- No new permissions or secrets.
- No dependency, package, or CI changes.
- No change to the commands themselves; only the Telegram Bot API scopes used to set and clear native command menus changed.

## Verification

Verified on rebased head `9412fb790918841a1a7f50c23a7b377e7f2e5bc1` against base `84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775`.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts` passed: 2 files, 51 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.

## Real Behavior Proof

I ran the production `syncTelegramMenuCommands` path with a local Bot API capture adapter. The branch emitted command-menu calls in this order:

```text
deleteMyCommands: default scope
deleteMyCommands: all_private_chats
deleteMyCommands: all_group_chats
setMyCommands: default scope
setMyCommands: all_private_chats
setMyCommands: all_group_chats
```

The explicit native-disabled cleanup path now also clears:

```ts
setMyCommands([], { scope: { type: "all_private_chats" } })
```

## Known Limits

- Live Telegram Bot API or Telegram Desktop proof was not run in this workspace because no real Telegram bot token/session is available here.
- The proof covers OpenClaw's emitted Bot API method/options sequence and focused regression tests, not Telegram's remote UI rendering.

## Review Status

- No inline review threads remain open.
- ClawSweeper may still ask for live Telegram proof or an explicit maintainer proof override before merge.
